problems.title: Complete Human Proteome Structure Prediction
problems.tagline: Predict every human protein complex, not just single chains.
problems.summary: Extend AlphaFold-level accuracy to multi-chain complexes, dynamics, and PTMs.
problems.description: AlphaFold2 and AlphaFold3 have solved single-chain structure prediction. Open challenges remain: multi-chain complexes, conformational dynamics, intrinsically disordered regions, and post-translational modification effects. Completing the proteome structurally would transform molecular medicine.
problems.abstract: This project extends AlphaFold-style methods to full proteome coverage including dynamics and PTMs. Work includes (1) training on large-scale cryo-EM complex datasets for multimer accuracy, (2) diffusion-based ensemble prediction for flexible and disordered proteins, and (3) PTM-aware models accounting for phosphorylation, glycosylation, and ubiquitination. A completed dynamic proteome would underpin rational drug design.

problems.title: Mechanisms of Antibiotic Resistance Spread
problems.tagline: Map the real-time global spread of antibiotic resistance genes.
problems.summary: Enable real-time surveillance of resistance gene transmission via genomic epidemiology.
problems.description: Plasmid transfer and horizontal gene transfer drive antibiotic resistance spread. Genomic epidemiology enables real-time surveillance. Complete understanding would enable targeted interventions to delay resistance emergence.
problems.abstract: This project advances AMR surveillance via (1) global-scale genomic epidemiology pipelines integrating hospital and environmental data, (2) ML models predicting resistance emergence hotspots, and (3) intervention modeling for stewardship policies. Progress addresses a critical public health threat.

problems.title: CAR-T Cell Therapy for Solid Tumors
problems.tagline: Extend CAR-T's blood-cancer success to solid tumors.
problems.summary: Overcome the solid tumor microenvironment barrier to CAR-T therapy.
problems.description: CAR-T cells have cured relapsed blood cancers but fail in solid tumors due to immunosuppressive microenvironment, antigen heterogeneity, and trafficking barriers. Armored CARs, checkpoint combinations, and logic gates address these.
problems.abstract: We target solid tumor CAR-T via (1) armored CAR constructs secreting cytokines and checkpoint blockers, (2) dual-antigen logic-gated CARs avoiding off-tumor toxicity, and (3) tumor microenvironment reprogramming combinations. Success would revolutionize cancer immunotherapy.

problems.title: Complete Human Cell Atlas
problems.tagline: Catalog every cell type in the human body.
problems.summary: Complete mapping of all human cell types via single-cell and spatial omics.
problems.description: The Human Cell Atlas consortium has mapped ~50% of expected human cell types using single-cell RNA-seq and spatial transcriptomics. Complete coverage and integration with functional data would provide a cellular reference for all medicine.
problems.abstract: This project pursues Human Cell Atlas completion via (1) extending single-cell and spatial omics to underrepresented tissues, (2) multi-modal integration (protein, chromatin, metabolic), and (3) functional annotation of discovered cell types. A complete atlas anchors precision medicine.

problems.title: CRISPR Off-Target Prediction
problems.tagline: Predict CRISPR off-target edits with clinical-grade accuracy.
problems.summary: Achieve comprehensive prediction of CRISPR off-target effects for clinical use.
problems.description: Machine learning models for CRISPR off-target prediction are improving rapidly. Base editors and prime editors reduce off-target rates. Clinical-grade prediction enabling safe therapeutic editing is within reach.
problems.abstract: We target CRISPR safety via (1) deep learning off-target prediction trained on genome-wide assays, (2) base/prime editor specificity improvements, and (3) in silico design-review pipelines for clinical applications. Progress enables broader gene therapy deployment.

problems.title: Microbiome-Disease Causal Mechanisms
problems.tagline: Establish causal links between microbiome and disease.
problems.summary: Move from microbiome-disease correlations to causal mechanisms.
problems.description: Microbiome-disease correlations are established for many conditions but causality is often unclear. Gnotobiotic mouse models combined with metabolomics are closing the causality gap. Causal insights would enable microbiome-based therapeutics.
problems.abstract: This project targets microbiome causality via (1) gnotobiotic colonization experiments with defined communities, (2) metabolomic tracing of microbial products through host biology, and (3) Mendelian randomization in human cohorts. Progress enables rational microbiome therapeutics.

problems.title: mRNA Vaccines for Non-Infectious Diseases
problems.tagline: Extend mRNA vaccine success to cancer and autoimmune disease.
problems.summary: Optimize mRNA vaccine platforms for cancer neoantigens and tolerance induction.
problems.description: COVID-19 validated mRNA vaccines. Cancer neoantigen vaccines are in Phase III trials. Delivery optimization, antigen selection, and tolerance induction remain active challenges.
problems.abstract: We extend mRNA vaccines via (1) personalized neoantigen prediction pipelines, (2) lipid nanoparticle optimization for tissue targeting, and (3) tolerogenic mRNA for autoimmune disease. Progress expands mRNA platforms beyond infectious disease.

problems.title: Complete Catalog of Human Genetic Variants
problems.tagline: Catalog every human genetic variant across populations.
problems.summary: Complete cataloging of human genetic variation including structural variants.
problems.description: gnomAD, UK Biobank, and All of Us are building massive variant catalogs. Long-read sequencing is resolving structural variants previously invisible. Completion would anchor population genetics and clinical variant interpretation.
problems.abstract: This project advances variant cataloging via (1) long-read sequencing at population scale, (2) structural variant annotation pipelines, and (3) population-diverse cohort inclusion. A complete catalog enables precision medicine globally.

problems.title: Mechanisms of CRISPR Immune Evasion
problems.tagline: Map the bacterial-phage CRISPR arms race.
problems.summary: Fully characterize anti-CRISPR mechanisms and their therapeutic exploitation.
problems.description: Anti-CRISPR proteins discovered in phages provide off-switches for CRISPR tools. The bacterial-phage CRISPR arms race is well-documented. Therapeutic implications include controlling CRISPR editing duration and location.
problems.abstract: We pursue CRISPR immune evasion via (1) systematic discovery of anti-CRISPR proteins via metagenomics, (2) structural characterization of Acr-Cas complexes, and (3) engineering Acr-Cas toggles for therapeutic applications. Progress enables precise CRISPR control.

problems.title: Protein Folding Dynamics
problems.tagline: Understand conformational ensembles, not just static structures.
problems.summary: Characterize protein folding pathways, ensembles, and intrinsically disordered proteins.
problems.description: AlphaFold solved static structures. Protein dynamics — conformational ensembles, folding pathways, and intrinsically disordered proteins (IDPs) — remain challenging. Understanding function requires this dynamic view.
problems.abstract: This project pursues protein dynamics via (1) enhanced sampling MD reaching biological timescales, (2) generative models of IDP ensembles trained on NMR/SAXS data, and (3) machine learning prediction of folding pathways. Progress enables function-based drug design.

problems.title: Complete Human Cell Gene Regulatory Network
problems.tagline: Decode the full enhancer logic of human cells.
problems.summary: Map the complete gene regulatory network including 3D genome organization and phase separation.
problems.description: Transcription factor binding is well-mapped. Enhancer logic, 3D genome organization, and phase separation roles are being decoded. A complete regulatory network for one human cell type would be a landmark.
problems.abstract: We pursue GRN mapping via (1) massively parallel reporter assays for enhancer function, (2) Hi-C and microscopy-based 3D genome characterization, and (3) deep learning integration of multi-omic signals. Progress enables rational cell reprogramming.

problems.title: Mechanism of General Anesthesia
problems.tagline: Explain how anesthetics produce unconsciousness.
problems.summary: Identify the molecular and network mechanisms of general anesthesia.
problems.description: Lipid vs protein theories of anesthesia have been debated for 150 years. Ion channel mechanisms (GABA-A, NMDA, K2P) are emerging. Network-level effects on consciousness remain to be integrated.
problems.abstract: This project pursues anesthesia mechanism via (1) structural biology of anesthetic-channel complexes, (2) whole-brain imaging of anesthesia onset and offset, and (3) computational models linking molecular to network effects. Progress informs both neuroscience and clinical anesthesia.

problems.title: Why Do We Sleep?
problems.tagline: Identify sleep's core biological functions.
problems.summary: Establish a unifying theory of sleep's biological roles.
problems.description: Sleep clears brain metabolites via the glymphatic system, consolidates memories, and enables synaptic homeostasis. No unifying theory encompasses all sleep functions across species and contexts.
problems.abstract: We pursue sleep theory via (1) comparative analysis of sleep across species, (2) longitudinal studies of sleep disruption effects, and (3) integrated models of clearance, memory, and homeostasis. Progress addresses a fundamental biological puzzle.

problems.title: Gut-Brain Axis Signaling Map
problems.tagline: Map all gut-brain signaling pathways.
problems.summary: Complete the mapping of vagal, metabolic, and immune gut-brain signaling pathways.
problems.description: Vagus nerve, microbial metabolites, and immune mediators transmit gut signals to the brain. Complete pathway mapping is in progress and will enable microbiome-based neuropsychiatric therapeutics.
problems.abstract: This project maps the gut-brain axis via (1) vagal afferent single-cell transcriptomics, (2) metabolomic tracing of microbial products to brain, and (3) causal interventions in gnotobiotic models. Progress informs microbiome psychiatry.

problems.title: Senescence Reversal for Healthy Aging
problems.tagline: Reverse cellular senescence to extend healthspan.
problems.summary: Develop senolytic and partial-reprogramming therapies for aging.
problems.description: Senolytics (dasatinib + quercetin) are in clinical trials. Yamanaka factor partial reprogramming rejuvenates cells in vivo. Safe, durable senescence reversal could add healthy decades to human life.
problems.abstract: We target senescence reversal via (1) next-generation senolytic screening against diverse senescent cell populations, (2) controlled partial reprogramming with tunable expression, and (3) clinical trials measuring healthspan endpoints. Progress transforms aging medicine.

problems.title: De Novo Genome Design Principles
problems.tagline: Design genomes from scratch for specific functions.
problems.summary: Develop principles for designing functional genomes de novo.
problems.description: JCVI-syn3.0 represents a minimal bacterial genome (473 genes). Yeast Sc2.0 is near completion. Design principles for custom genomes with new functions are emerging from these efforts.
problems.abstract: This project pursues genome design via (1) minimal essential gene sets across diverse organisms, (2) modular synthetic circuit integration into genome backbones, and (3) predictive models for emergent properties. Progress enables programmable organisms.

problems.title: Universal Flu Vaccine
problems.tagline: One shot protecting against all influenza strains.
problems.summary: Develop a universal influenza vaccine targeting conserved epitopes.
problems.description: Stalk-targeting antibodies and mosaic nanoparticle vaccines target conserved flu epitopes. Phase II/III trials are ongoing. A successful universal vaccine would end annual flu shots and prepare for pandemics.
problems.abstract: We pursue universal flu vaccines via (1) structure-guided immunogen design for conserved HA stalk, (2) computationally designed mosaic nanoparticle platforms, and (3) correlates-of-protection studies. Progress enables pandemic preparedness.

problems.title: Complete Human Brain Connectome
problems.tagline: Map every synapse in a human brain.
problems.summary: Achieve nanoscale synaptic connectivity mapping of a human brain.
problems.description: C. elegans (302 neurons) and Drosophila larva (2023) connectomes are complete. Scaling to human (86 billion neurons) requires new imaging and reconstruction technology. Multi-decade effort but scalable.
problems.abstract: This project advances human connectomics via (1) automated serial-section EM at multi-cm³ scale, (2) ML-based segmentation and synapse detection, and (3) multi-modal integration with function. Progress toward human connectome reshapes neuroscience.

problems.title: Mechanisms of Metastasis
problems.tagline: Understand and prevent cancer metastasis.
problems.summary: Elucidate metastasis mechanisms and develop prevention strategies.
problems.description: Epithelial-mesenchymal transition, circulating tumor cells, and pre-metastatic niches drive metastasis. Tissue tropism is partially explained. Prevention strategies remain limited despite metastasis causing most cancer deaths.
problems.abstract: We target metastasis via (1) single-cell analysis of CTCs and disseminated tumor cells, (2) pre-metastatic niche characterization, and (3) drug screens blocking metastatic steps. Progress reduces cancer mortality.

problems.title: Prion-Like Propagation in Neurodegeneration
problems.tagline: Halt template-directed misfolding in Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.
problems.summary: Intercept prion-like spread of misfolded proteins in neurodegenerative diseases.
problems.description: Tau, α-synuclein, and TDP-43 spread between neurons in prion-like fashion. Template-directed misfolding drives disease progression. Therapeutic interception at cell-to-cell transmission is an active area.
problems.abstract: This project pursues prion-like intercept via (1) structural characterization of seeding species, (2) antibody and small molecule inhibitors of propagation, and (3) in vivo tracking of spread in mouse models. Progress treats neurodegeneration at its mechanism.

problems.title: Epigenetic Inheritance Mechanisms
problems.tagline: How do epigenetic marks transmit across generations?
problems.summary: Characterize mechanisms of transgenerational epigenetic inheritance.
problems.description: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance is documented in C. elegans, plants, and mammals. Mechanisms — piRNA, chromatin marks, metabolites — are being dissected. Implications span evolution and disease risk.
problems.abstract: We pursue epigenetic inheritance via (1) piRNA pathway analysis across generations, (2) chromatin mark propagation through germline reprogramming, and (3) metabolite-based signaling studies. Progress reshapes genetics and evolution.

problems.title: Synthetic Cell from Scratch
problems.tagline: Build a self-dividing synthetic cell bottom-up.
problems.summary: Construct a fully synthetic self-sustaining dividing cell.
problems.description: Bottom-up synthetic biology combines lipid vesicles, minimal gene sets, and metabolism. Self-sustaining division has not been achieved. Success would constitute creating life from non-living components.
problems.abstract: This project pursues synthetic cells via (1) minimal genome installation into vesicles, (2) coupled metabolism and replication systems, and (3) self-division mechanisms. Progress probes the chemistry-biology boundary.

problems.title: Complete Human Metabolome
problems.tagline: Identify every metabolite in the human body.
problems.summary: Annotate the full human metabolome including the 'dark metabolome'.
problems.description: HMDB contains ~220,000 entries but ~40% of detected blood metabolites remain unidentified ('dark metabolome'). Completing this catalog would enable comprehensive metabolic biomarker discovery.
problems.abstract: We pursue human metabolome completion via (1) multimodal MS and NMR across tissues, (2) ML-assisted structural annotation for unknown features, and (3) population-scale metabolomic cohort integration. Progress enables precision metabolic medicine.

problems.title: Immunotherapy for Autoimmune Diseases
problems.tagline: Antigen-specific tolerance for autoimmunity.
problems.summary: Develop antigen-specific tolerance induction therapies for autoimmune diseases.
problems.description: Regulatory T cell therapy and antigen-specific tolerance induction are being explored. CAR-Treg cells are in early trials. Success would replace broad immunosuppression with targeted tolerance.
problems.abstract: This project pursues autoimmune immunotherapy via (1) CAR-Treg engineering for autoantigen specificity, (2) tolerogenic nanoparticle vaccines, and (3) microbiome interventions inducing systemic tolerance. Progress transforms autoimmune treatment.

problems.title: Cure for Alzheimer's Disease
problems.tagline: Develop disease-modifying Alzheimer's therapy.
problems.summary: Identify and target the full mechanism of Alzheimer's disease for cure.
problems.description: Amyloid-targeting drugs (lecanemab) validated the amyloid hypothesis partially. Tau pathology, neuroinflammation, and vascular factors contribute. Multi-target strategies are likely needed for a true cure.
problems.abstract: We pursue Alzheimer's cure via (1) multi-target combination therapies (amyloid + tau + inflammation), (2) early intervention before symptom onset, and (3) lifestyle and genetic risk integration. Progress addresses the largest neurodegeneration burden.

problems.title: Comprehensive Cancer Cure
problems.tagline: Cure all cancers, not just selected types.
problems.summary: Develop broadly effective cancer therapies overcoming heterogeneity and resistance.
problems.description: Cancer is over 200 diseases. Immunotherapy works for some. Tumor heterogeneity, clonal evolution, and resistance are fundamental barriers. A comprehensive cure requires addressing these systematically.
problems.abstract: This project pursues comprehensive cancer cure via (1) universal immunotherapy targets (WT1, surviving, shared neoantigens), (2) evolution-predictive adaptive therapy, and (3) early cancer detection enabling curative intervention. Progress would be the ultimate medical achievement.

problems.title: HIV Functional Cure
problems.tagline: Eliminate the HIV latent reservoir for a functional cure.
problems.summary: Develop strategies to eliminate or permanently silence the HIV latent reservoir.
problems.description: ART controls HIV but requires lifelong treatment due to latent reservoir. Shock-and-kill, block-and-lock, and gene therapy (CCR5 disruption) approaches are in clinical trials. A functional cure remains elusive.
problems.abstract: We pursue HIV cure via (1) latency-reversing agents combined with immune clearance, (2) CRISPR-based provirus excision, and (3) broadly neutralizing antibody maintenance. Progress would end lifelong HIV therapy.

problems.title: Complete Gene Regulation Understanding
problems.tagline: Decode the complete logic of gene regulation.
problems.summary: Unify understanding of transcriptional, chromatin, and RNA-level gene regulation.
problems.description: Enhancers, silencers, insulators, 3D chromatin, phase separation, and RNA regulation combine into enormous combinatorial complexity. A complete understanding would enable rational cell reprogramming.
problems.abstract: This project pursues gene regulation decoding via (1) massively parallel reporter assays across cell types, (2) 4D nucleome dynamics imaging, and (3) deep learning integration models. Progress impacts all of biology.

problems.title: Neural Code
problems.tagline: How does the brain encode information?
problems.summary: Identify the fundamental units of neural information encoding.
problems.description: Rate coding, temporal coding, and population coding have all been observed. A unified framework for neural coding remains missing. Resolution would enable brain-computer interfaces and artificial brains.
problems.abstract: We pursue the neural code via (1) large-scale neural recording during natural behavior, (2) information-theoretic analysis of coding schemes, and (3) causal perturbations testing coding hypotheses. Progress reshapes neuroscience.

problems.title: Mechanism of Morphogenesis
problems.tagline: Explain how shape emerges in developing embryos.
problems.summary: Integrate chemical and mechanical signaling in morphogenesis.
problems.description: Turing patterns, morphogen gradients, and mechanotransduction collectively shape embryos. Integration of chemical and mechanical signaling into predictive models of morphogenesis remains a frontier.
problems.abstract: This project pursues morphogenesis via (1) multi-scale mechanochemical models validated on organoids, (2) high-resolution imaging of signaling dynamics, and (3) machine learning of shape generation. Progress enables rational tissue engineering.

problems.title: Origin of Eukaryotic Cell
problems.tagline: Explain how the eukaryotic cell arose.
problems.summary: Elucidate the origins of nucleus, mitochondria, and endomembrane system.
problems.description: Asgard archaea discovered in 2015 as likely hosts. Endosymbiosis of α-proteobacterium gave mitochondria. Origins of nucleus and endomembranes remain debated. Key to understanding complex life.
problems.abstract: We pursue eukaryote origins via (1) cultivation and characterization of Asgard archaea, (2) comparative genomics of archaeal-bacterial chimeras, and (3) evolutionary reconstruction of early eukaryotic cell biology. Progress illuminates life's major transition.

problems.title: Biological Basis of Consciousness
problems.tagline: Identify the neural correlates and mechanisms of consciousness.
problems.summary: Experimentally distinguish competing theories of consciousness.
problems.description: Neural correlates of consciousness (NCC) are identified. IIT, Global Workspace Theory, and Higher-Order Theories offer competing frameworks. Experimental distinction is challenging but progressing.
problems.abstract: This project pursues consciousness mechanisms via (1) adversarial collaboration between theories, (2) perturbational complexity index measurements, and (3) high-resolution human brain recordings during conscious transitions. Progress informs one of science's hardest questions.

problems.title: Mechanism of Biological Aging
problems.tagline: Unify the hallmarks of aging into a predictive framework.
problems.summary: Integrate the 12 hallmarks of aging into a unified mechanistic theory.
problems.description: López-Otín's hallmarks of aging (2013, updated 2023 to 12) catalog aging processes but their interactions remain poorly understood. A unified theory would enable targeted aging interventions.
problems.abstract: We pursue unified aging theory via (1) longitudinal multi-omics of aging across tissues, (2) causal perturbation studies of hallmark interactions, and (3) mathematical models integrating damage and repair. Progress enables aging medicine.

problems.title: Complete Understanding of the Immune System
problems.tagline: Integrate all components of immunity into a unified model.
problems.summary: Unify adaptive, innate, trained, and tissue-resident immunity.
problems.description: Adaptive and innate immunity are well-characterized individually. Integration, trained immunity, tissue-resident memory, and immune aging remain challenging. A complete integration would transform immunotherapy.
problems.abstract: This project pursues unified immunity via (1) high-dimensional phenotyping across tissues, (2) integrated models of innate-adaptive crosstalk, and (3) aging and trained immunity mechanisms. Progress enables systems-level immunotherapy.

problems.title: Whole-Brain Emulation
problems.tagline: Simulate a full human brain with biological fidelity.
problems.summary: Achieve whole-brain emulation combining nanoscale connectomics and functional dynamics.
problems.description: OpenWorm (302 neurons) is partially complete. Scaling to human (86 billion neurons) requires nanoscale connectomics and biophysically detailed simulation. Decades-long project but roadmap is clear.
problems.abstract: We pursue whole-brain emulation via (1) scaled automated connectomics, (2) exascale computing for biophysical simulation, and (3) validation via behavioral prediction. Progress would constitute a transformative achievement.

problems.title: De-Extinction of Species
problems.tagline: Bring extinct species back to life.
problems.summary: Resurrect extinct species via CRISPR and ancient DNA.
problems.description: Colossal Biosciences pursues woolly mammoth and thylacine de-extinction via CRISPR editing. Ecological reintegration and ethical questions accompany technical challenges. Progress tests the limits of synthetic biology.
problems.abstract: This project pursues de-extinction via (1) ancient DNA recovery and assembly, (2) multiplexed CRISPR editing of closely related species, and (3) ecological assessment for reintroduction. Progress tests ambitious biology.

problems.title: Predictive Ecology
problems.tagline: Predict ecosystem dynamics under environmental change.
problems.summary: Build predictive models of ecosystem dynamics and tipping points.
problems.description: Ecosystem modeling remains primitive. Tipping points are poorly predicted. Biodiversity-function relationships are complex. Progress would enable proactive conservation.
problems.abstract: We pursue predictive ecology via (1) data-driven dynamic models trained on long-term ecosystem time series, (2) early warning signals for tipping points, and (3) experimental validation in microcosms. Progress supports conservation and climate adaptation.

problems.title: Complete Human Developmental Program
problems.tagline: Map human development from zygote to adult.
problems.summary: Complete characterization of the human developmental program.
problems.description: Gastruloids and stem cell models advance human development research despite ethical constraints (14-day rule). Complete mapping would illuminate birth defects and regenerative medicine.
problems.abstract: This project pursues human development mapping via (1) advanced stem-cell-based embryo models, (2) single-cell atlases across gestational timepoints, and (3) multi-organ morphogenesis in bioprinted tissues. Progress informs developmental medicine.

problems.title: Long-Term Memory Storage Mechanism
problems.tagline: How is long-term memory stored in neurons?
problems.summary: Identify the molecular substrate of long-term memory.
problems.description: Synaptic plasticity (LTP/LTD) is necessary but not sufficient for long-term memory. Engram cells, prion-like mechanisms, and epigenetic marks are candidates. Resolution would inform memory enhancement and neurodegeneration.
problems.abstract: We pursue memory storage via (1) engram cell manipulation experiments, (2) CPEB-like prion mechanisms in synapses, and (3) epigenetic memory marks. Progress addresses a fundamental neuroscience question.

problems.title: Complete Protein-Protein Interaction Network
problems.tagline: Map all human protein-protein interactions.
problems.summary: Complete human protein-protein interactome including context specificity.
problems.description: ~650,000 human PPIs are known; the estimated total exceeds 1,000,000. Context-dependent and tissue-specific interactions remain largely uncharacterized. Complete coverage enables network medicine.
problems.abstract: This project pursues PPI completion via (1) high-throughput Y2H and AP-MS across tissues, (2) structural prediction of interfaces via AlphaFold-Multimer, and (3) integration into tissue-specific interactomes. Progress enables network-based drug discovery.

problems.title: Complete Virome of Earth
problems.tagline: Catalog all viruses on Earth.
problems.summary: Complete cataloging of Earth's estimated 10³¹ viral particles.
problems.description: Estimated 10³¹ viral particles exist globally; less than 1% are characterized. Metagenomics is rapidly expanding coverage but analysis is bottlenecked. A complete virome would inform pandemic preparedness.
problems.abstract: We pursue virome characterization via (1) metagenomic sampling across Earth's ecosystems, (2) ML-based novel virus identification, and (3) functional annotation via protein structure prediction. Progress supports pandemic preparedness.

problems.title: Mammalian Organ Regeneration
problems.tagline: Regenerate organs in humans as salamanders do limbs.
problems.summary: Enable full organ regeneration in mammals.
problems.description: Salamanders regenerate limbs fully. Mammalian regeneration is limited (liver partial). MRL mouse strain shows enhanced regeneration. Hippo, Wnt, and mTOR pathways are involved. A predictive framework could enable human organ regeneration.
problems.abstract: This project pursues organ regeneration via (1) comparative analysis of regeneration across species, (2) pathway manipulation in mouse injury models, and (3) organoid and bioprinted tissue generation. Progress transforms regenerative medicine.

problems.title: Synthetic Minimal Ecosystem
problems.tagline: Design the minimum viable closed ecosystem.
problems.summary: Build a self-sustaining minimal ecosystem for space or as a model.
problems.description: Biosphere 2 famously failed. Closed ecological life support systems are key for space colonization. The minimum viable ecosystem is unknown. Progress informs sustainability and space biology.
problems.abstract: We pursue synthetic ecosystems via (1) microcosm design with defined species assemblages, (2) long-duration closed-system experiments, and (3) ecological theory guiding minimal design. Progress enables sustainable closed systems.

problems.title: Post-Antibiotic Era Alternatives
problems.tagline: Replace antibiotics as resistance expands.
problems.summary: Develop phage therapy, antimicrobial peptides, and anti-virulence strategies.
problems.description: Antibiotic resistance threatens modern medicine. Alternatives include phage therapy, antimicrobial peptides, CRISPR-Cas antimicrobials, and anti-virulence strategies. No single solution but combinations are promising.
problems.abstract: This project pursues antibiotic alternatives via (1) personalized phage therapy protocols, (2) host-directed therapies reducing virulence, and (3) CRISPR-Cas antimicrobials targeting resistance genes. Progress addresses a critical threat.

problems.title: Complete Tree of Life
problems.tagline: Catalog every species on Earth.
problems.summary: Complete the tree of life across all domains.
problems.description: ~2 million species described; estimated 8-10 million total. Microbial dark matter dominates the unknown. Metagenomics and environmental DNA are closing the gap.
problems.abstract: We pursue tree of life completion via (1) environmental DNA sampling at global scale, (2) ML-based species delimitation, and (3) phylogenetic methods for handling incomplete data. Progress catalogs Earth's biodiversity.

problems.title: Brain-Computer Interface Full Communication
problems.tagline: Direct neural communication of speech, thought, and emotion.
problems.summary: Achieve natural communication bandwidth through BCIs.
problems.description: Neuralink, BrainGate, and similar systems decode motor intent effectively. Speech, thought, and emotion decoding remain limited. Full-bandwidth communication would transform disability care and human-machine interaction.
problems.abstract: This project pursues BCI advances via (1) high-density electrode arrays for speech motor cortex, (2) deep learning decoders for natural language, and (3) integration with closed-loop feedback. Progress restores communication for locked-in patients.

problems.title: Complete Understanding of RNA World
problems.tagline: How did RNA-based life arise?
problems.summary: Reconstruct the RNA world's origins and transition to DNA/protein life.
problems.description: Ribozyme catalysis and RNA replication support the RNA world hypothesis. How RNA arose from prebiotic chemistry and how it transitioned to DNA/protein life remain open.
problems.abstract: We pursue the RNA world via (1) directed evolution of RNA replicases, (2) prebiotic syntheses of nucleotides, and (3) protocell models with RNA metabolism. Progress addresses life's origins.

problems.title: Xenotransplantation
problems.tagline: Transplant pig organs into humans safely.
problems.summary: Develop durable, immunologically compatible xenotransplants.
problems.description: Gene-edited pig kidney transplanted into humans in 2024. Immune rejection, PERV (porcine endogenous retrovirus), and long-term function remain open. Success would solve the organ shortage.
problems.abstract: This project pursues xenotransplantation via (1) multi-gene edited pig donors eliminating xenoantigens, (2) immunosuppression tailored to pig-human mismatches, and (3) long-term function monitoring. Progress addresses organ shortage.

problems.title: Complete Pharmacogenomics
problems.tagline: Personalize drug response based on genome.
problems.summary: Achieve complete pharmacogenomic understanding for personalized medicine.
problems.description: CYP2D6 and CYP2C19 are well-characterized. Polygenic drug response profiles are mapping drug efficacy and toxicity to genetic variants. Full personalized medicine remains distant.
problems.abstract: We pursue pharmacogenomics via (1) population-scale drug response cohort studies, (2) polygenic risk scores for adverse drug reactions, and (3) clinical decision support integration. Progress transforms prescribing.

problems.title: Origin of Life
problems.tagline: Explain how life arose from non-living matter.
problems.summary: Establish the chemical and environmental pathway from non-living matter to life.
problems.description: RNA world, metabolism-first, clay hypothesis, and hydrothermal vents compete as origin scenarios. Miller-Urey (1953) started the field. No consensus exists, despite decades of progress.
problems.abstract: This project pursues origin of life via (1) systematic comparison of origin scenarios against prebiotic chemistry constraints, (2) hydrothermal vent laboratory analogs, and (3) minimal self-replicating systems. Progress addresses biology's deepest question.

problems.title: How Did the Genetic Code Evolve?
problems.tagline: Explain the 64-codon to 20-amino-acid mapping.
problems.summary: Determine whether the genetic code is frozen accident, stereochemical, or co-evolved.
problems.description: The genetic code maps 64 codons to 20 amino acids with remarkable universality. Frozen accident, stereochemical affinity, and co-evolution hypotheses each have support. Resolution illuminates life's origin.
problems.abstract: We pursue genetic code origins via (1) experimental tests of codon-amino-acid stereochemistry, (2) evolutionary simulation of alternative codes, and (3) comparative analysis of modified codes in nature. Progress informs life's origin.

problems.title: Biological Basis of Intelligence
problems.tagline: Explain what makes brains intelligent.
problems.summary: Identify the biological mechanisms underlying cognitive ability differences.
problems.description: IQ is ~50-80% heritable. GWAS identifies thousands of small-effect variants. Whether intelligence arises from neural efficiency, connectivity, or something else remains open.
problems.abstract: This project pursues intelligence biology via (1) integration of GWAS variants into neural developmental pathways, (2) imaging studies linking brain structure to cognitive ability, and (3) comparative analysis across species. Progress informs education and neurology.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Evolution
problems.tagline: Integrate modern synthesis with extended evolutionary framework.
problems.summary: Develop a unified evolutionary theory including epigenetics, niche construction, and evo-devo.
problems.description: Modern synthesis + neutral theory form the current core. Extended evolutionary synthesis adds evo-devo, niche construction, and epigenetics. Whether these are extensions or revolutions is debated.
problems.abstract: We pursue unified evolutionary theory via (1) systematic incorporation of epigenetic inheritance into population genetics, (2) niche construction models, and (3) empirical tests of extended synthesis predictions. Progress updates biology's foundation.

problems.title: Mechanisms of Speciation
problems.tagline: Explain how new species arise.
problems.summary: Characterize genomic mechanisms of speciation including sympatric cases.
problems.description: Allopatric speciation is well-understood. Sympatric speciation mechanisms are debated. Genomic incompatibilities and reproductive isolation mechanisms are complex.
problems.abstract: This project pursues speciation mechanisms via (1) genome scans for speciation genes across diverse taxa, (2) experimental evolution tests, and (3) theoretical models of reproductive isolation. Progress addresses evolution's core process.

problems.title: Reverse Aging
problems.tagline: Rejuvenate whole organisms, not just cells.
problems.summary: Achieve systemic aging reversal via reprogramming.
problems.description: Yamanaka factors rejuvenate cells in vitro and in vivo. Whole-organism reversal requires safe, targeted reprogramming avoiding cancer risk. Progress could transform healthspan.
problems.abstract: We pursue whole-organism rejuvenation via (1) cyclic transient Yamanaka factor expression, (2) safer reprogramming factor combinations, and (3) tissue-specific delivery strategies. Progress enables unprecedented healthspan extension.

problems.title: General Artificial Photosynthesis
problems.tagline: Match natural photosynthesis efficiency in an artificial system.
problems.summary: Develop robust artificial photosynthesis integrating water splitting, CO₂ fixation, and fuel storage.
problems.description: Natural photosynthesis is ~1% efficient in real conditions. Artificial systems improve but are fragile. Integration of water splitting, CO₂ fixation, and fuel storage is the challenge.
problems.abstract: This project pursues artificial photosynthesis via (1) robust earth-abundant catalysts, (2) integration with CO₂ fixation and fuel synthesis, and (3) large-scale demonstration. Progress enables renewable fuels.

problems.title: Complete Soil Microbiome Function
problems.tagline: Understand soil's microbial universe.
problems.summary: Characterize all soil microbes and their functions.
problems.description: Soil contains ~10⁹ bacteria per gram, driving carbon and nitrogen cycles. Most species are unculturable. Complete functional understanding would inform agriculture and climate.
problems.abstract: We pursue soil microbiome function via (1) metagenomic and metatranscriptomic characterization, (2) cultivation of novel species, and (3) functional assays tied to ecosystem services. Progress informs agriculture and climate.

problems.title: Major Evolutionary Transitions
problems.tagline: Explain replicators → cells → eukaryotes → multicellularity → sociality.
problems.summary: Characterize mechanisms of the major evolutionary transitions.
problems.description: Each major transition (Maynard Smith-Szathmáry) is poorly understood mechanistically. Questions of how cooperation overcame conflict are central. Progress illuminates life's complexity ladder.
problems.abstract: This project pursues major transitions via (1) experimental evolution of multicellularity (Snowflake yeast), (2) comparative genomics across transitions, and (3) theoretical frameworks for cooperation evolution. Progress clarifies biology's biggest events.

problems.title: Directed Evolution of Complex Traits
problems.tagline: Evolve complex pathways and circuits by design.
problems.summary: Extend directed evolution from single enzymes to complete pathways and circuits.
problems.description: Single-enzyme directed evolution (Frances Arnold, Nobel 2018) works well. Pathways and regulatory circuits are much harder. Progress would enable custom biosynthesis.
problems.abstract: We pursue complex directed evolution via (1) genome-wide mutagenesis with high-throughput selection, (2) ML-guided search in pathway space, and (3) modular circuit evolution. Progress enables biosynthetic engineering.

problems.title: Complete Ocean Microbiome Map
problems.tagline: Catalog ocean microbial diversity and function.
problems.summary: Complete the ocean microbiome atlas with functional annotation.
problems.description: Tara Oceans expedition mapped microbial diversity across oceans. Estimated millions of species exist. Functional roles remain mostly unknown. Progress informs climate and biogeochemistry.
problems.abstract: This project pursues ocean microbiome mapping via (1) expanded global sampling, (2) metagenomic functional annotation, and (3) linkage to ocean biogeochemical cycles. Progress informs Earth system science.

problems.title: Neural Basis of Free Will
problems.tagline: Does neuroscience determine free will's existence?
problems.summary: Examine neural correlates of decision-making and their implications for free will.
problems.description: Libet experiments show readiness potentials preceding conscious decisions. The determinism vs agency debate is both philosophical and empirical. Progress informs ethics and law.
problems.abstract: We pursue free will neuroscience via (1) high-resolution decision-making neural imaging, (2) causal perturbation experiments, and (3) philosophical analysis of experimental implications. Progress bridges neuroscience and ethics.

problems.title: Evolution of Multicellularity
problems.tagline: Why did multicellular life evolve independently many times?
problems.summary: Characterize mechanisms driving independent evolutions of multicellularity.
problems.description: Multicellularity evolved independently over 25 times. Volvox, Dictyostelium, and experimental yeast provide models. Cell cooperation mechanisms and conflict suppression are central.
problems.abstract: This project pursues multicellularity evolution via (1) experimental evolution of snowflake yeast, (2) comparative genomics across multicellular transitions, and (3) theoretical cooperation models. Progress clarifies a major biological transition.

problems.title: Complete Understanding of Pain
problems.tagline: Understand and treat chronic pain mechanistically.
problems.summary: Unify peripheral and central pain mechanisms into therapeutic targets.
problems.description: Nociceptors and TRP channels are characterized. Central sensitization drives chronic pain. Mind-body interactions complicate the picture. Progress is critical to addressing the opioid crisis.
problems.abstract: We pursue pain mechanisms via (1) single-cell analysis of pain pathways, (2) central sensitization mechanisms in neural circuits, and (3) non-opioid therapy development. Progress addresses a major health burden.

problems.title: Prion Disease Cure
problems.tagline: Develop effective therapy for prion diseases.
problems.summary: Achieve effective therapy for fatal prion diseases.
problems.description: PrPSc structure is known but no effective treatment exists. Antisense oligonucleotide therapy (tofersen-like) is being explored. Progress addresses a uniformly fatal class of diseases.
problems.abstract: This project pursues prion therapy via (1) ASOs reducing PrP expression, (2) small molecule PrP stabilizers, and (3) immunotherapy against PrPSc. Progress addresses a uniformly fatal disease class.

problems.title: Cure for All Genetic Diseases
problems.tagline: Cure all ~7,000 rare genetic diseases.
problems.summary: Develop curative therapy for all monogenic and polygenic genetic diseases.
problems.description: CRISPR gene therapy is approved for sickle cell disease. Delivery to all tissues remains unsolved. ~7,000 rare diseases await therapy. Progress would transform medicine.
problems.abstract: We pursue genetic disease cure via (1) universal in vivo delivery platforms, (2) safer base and prime editors, and (3) platform approaches for rare disease therapy. Progress addresses vast unmet need.

problems.title: Universal Theory of Ecosystem Stability
problems.tagline: Why are complex ecosystems stable despite May's paradox?
problems.summary: Develop a universal theory of ecosystem stability.
problems.description: May's paradox states that complex random systems are unstable, yet real ecosystems persist. Network theory, redundancy, and modularity offer partial explanations. A universal theory remains elusive.
problems.abstract: This project pursues ecosystem stability via (1) network analysis of real food webs, (2) experimental stability tests in microcosms, and (3) theoretical frameworks linking structure and stability. Progress informs conservation.

problems.title: Full Artificial Womb
problems.tagline: Complete ectogenesis outside a biological womb.
problems.summary: Develop artificial wombs supporting full gestation.
problems.description: EXTEND system supports late-stage premature lamb gestation. Human application faces ethical, technical, and regulatory barriers. Progress would transform neonatal care and reproductive rights.
problems.abstract: We pursue artificial womb via (1) extended extracorporeal support systems, (2) hormonal and nutritional recapitulation of placenta, and (3) ethical frameworks for clinical translation. Progress addresses neonatal care.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Sexual Selection
problems.tagline: Unify sexual selection mechanisms.
problems.summary: Develop a unified theory of sexual selection.
problems.description: Fisher runaway, handicap principle, and sensory bias explain aspects of sexual selection. No unified theory exists. Mate choice mechanisms are complex and context-dependent.
problems.abstract: This project pursues sexual selection theory via (1) comparative analysis across diverse mating systems, (2) genetic dissection of choice mechanisms, and (3) theoretical synthesis of competing models. Progress clarifies a central evolutionary mechanism.

problems.title: Mechanism of Placebo Effect
problems.tagline: Explain and exploit the placebo effect therapeutically.
problems.summary: Characterize placebo mechanisms for clinical benefit.
problems.description: Endogenous opioids, dopamine, and prefrontal-brainstem circuits mediate placebo. Magnitude is variable and unpredictable. Clinical exploitation is limited. Progress could enhance treatment without drugs.
problems.abstract: We pursue placebo mechanisms via (1) imaging of placebo-responsive neural circuits, (2) molecular characterization of placebo effects, and (3) clinical protocols maximizing placebo benefit. Progress enhances therapeutic outcomes.

problems.title: Engineering Crop Nitrogen Fixation
problems.tagline: Engineer cereal crops to fix atmospheric nitrogen.
problems.summary: Transfer nitrogen fixation capability to cereal crops.
problems.description: Nitrogenase requires anaerobic conditions, complicating transfer to aerobic plant tissues. Engineering cereal nitrogen fixation would eliminate synthetic fertilizer needs and transform agriculture.
problems.abstract: This project pursues crop N fixation via (1) nitrogenase engineering for oxygen tolerance, (2) bacterial symbiont engineering in cereal rhizosphere, and (3) chloroplast-based fixation strategies. Progress transforms agriculture.

problems.title: Human Microbiome Development
problems.tagline: Map microbiome development from birth to stable adult community.
problems.summary: Characterize critical windows and influences on human microbiome development.
problems.description: The human microbiome develops from birth to stable adult community. Critical windows, antibiotic effects, and diet impact. Causality vs correlation remains challenging.
problems.abstract: We pursue microbiome development via (1) longitudinal birth cohorts with microbiome and metadata, (2) gnotobiotic studies of developmental windows, and (3) intervention trials in early life. Progress informs preventive medicine.

problems.title: Mechanism of Biological Self-Organization
problems.tagline: How does DNA encode robust self-organization?
problems.summary: Explain how genomes robustly encode self-organizing developmental programs.
problems.description: Turing patterns, reaction-diffusion systems, and mechanical feedback drive biological self-organization. How these robust patterns are encoded in the genome is incompletely understood.
problems.abstract: This project pursues self-organization theory via (1) mechanochemical models of embryo patterning, (2) organoid experiments testing predictions, and (3) genetic dissection of robustness. Progress addresses development's fundamentals.

problems.title: Human Artificial Chromosome
problems.tagline: Build stable human artificial chromosomes.
problems.summary: Achieve stable human artificial chromosomes for gene therapy.
problems.description: Yeast artificial chromosomes are well-established. Human artificial chromosomes remain unstable due to centromere biology challenges. Stable HACs would enable large-scale gene therapy.
problems.abstract: We pursue human artificial chromosomes via (1) characterization of functional centromere sequences, (2) stable minichromosome construction, and (3) delivery strategies for therapeutic applications. Progress enables large-payload gene therapy.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Cancer Evolution
problems.tagline: Predict cancer evolution for therapy.
problems.summary: Develop predictive models of clonal cancer evolution.
problems.description: Clonal evolution and branched phylogenies describe cancer progression. Predicting evolution trajectories for therapy selection remains challenging. Progress would enable adaptive therapy.
problems.abstract: This project pursues cancer evolution prediction via (1) longitudinal single-cell and liquid biopsy monitoring, (2) evolutionary dynamics models, and (3) adaptive therapy protocols. Progress transforms cancer management.

problems.title: What Is Life? (Formal Definition)
problems.tagline: Provide a universally accepted definition of life.
problems.summary: Establish a formal definition of life applicable across Earth and potentially beyond.
problems.description: Schrödinger's 1944 question remains unanswered. NASA's working definition ('self-sustaining chemical system capable of Darwinian evolution') is insufficient. A formal universal definition eludes biology.
problems.abstract: We pursue a formal definition of life via (1) systematic analysis of proposed definitions against biological examples, (2) information-theoretic criteria, and (3) astrobiological implications. Progress clarifies biology's subject matter.

problems.title: Hard Problem of Consciousness
problems.tagline: Why is there subjective experience at all?
problems.summary: Address the hard problem of explaining subjective experience.
problems.description: Chalmers (1995) posed the hard problem: why is there subjective experience? No physical theory addresses qualia. This may lie outside current scientific frameworks.
problems.abstract: This project examines the hard problem via (1) analysis of existing theories (IIT, GWT, HOT), (2) experimental proposals testing predictions, and (3) philosophical analysis of explanatory gap. Progress may transcend current science.

problems.title: Determinism of Biological Development
problems.tagline: Is development deterministic or stochastic?
problems.summary: Characterize the balance of deterministic and stochastic processes in development.
problems.description: Identical genomes produce similar but not identical organisms. How much is deterministic vs stochastic? Waddington's landscape offers a metaphor but no quantitative framework.
problems.abstract: We pursue development determinism via (1) twin studies with high-resolution phenotyping, (2) single-cell lineage tracing, and (3) mathematical models of stochasticity. Progress clarifies development's predictability.

problems.title: Can We Predict Evolution?
problems.tagline: Is evolution contingent or convergent?
problems.summary: Determine the predictability of evolutionary outcomes.
problems.description: Gould (contingency) vs Conway Morris (convergence) frame the debate. Lenski's long-term E. coli evolution shows both predictable and contingent outcomes. Full predictability may be impossible.
problems.abstract: This project pursues evolution predictability via (1) replay experiments in experimental evolution, (2) comparative analysis of convergence across lineages, and (3) theoretical limits on predictability. Progress addresses biology's contingency.

problems.title: Limits of Lifespan Extension
problems.tagline: Is there a fundamental upper bound on lifespan?
problems.summary: Determine whether biological lifespan has a fundamental upper bound.
problems.description: Hayflick limit, telomeres, and programmed aging theories compete with damage accumulation models. Whether aging is programmed or accidental affects intervention strategies and ultimate lifespan limits.
problems.abstract: We pursue lifespan limits via (1) comparative biology across exceptionally long-lived species, (2) mathematical models of damage and repair, and (3) extrapolation from current longevity data. Progress frames aging interventions.

problems.title: Sentience in Non-Neural Organisms
problems.tagline: Can plants, fungi, and slime molds be sentient?
problems.summary: Assess whether decision-making in non-neural organisms constitutes sentience.
problems.description: Plants, fungi, and slime molds exhibit decision-making behaviors without neurons. Whether this constitutes sentience depends on definitions. Progress informs philosophy of mind and AI.
problems.abstract: This project examines non-neural sentience via (1) behavioral analysis of decision-making in plants and fungi, (2) information integration in non-neural systems, and (3) philosophical analysis of sentience criteria. Progress broadens cognition concepts.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Development
problems.tagline: Map genome to morphology completely.
problems.summary: Develop a complete theory mapping genome to organismal morphology.
problems.description: From single cell to organism: the genome-to-morphology map is non-linear, high-dimensional, and context-dependent. A complete theory would predict morphology from sequence — analogous to protein folding but at the organism level.
problems.abstract: We pursue complete developmental theory via (1) integration of single-cell atlases with genetic perturbations, (2) mechanochemical models of organogenesis, and (3) deep learning predicting morphology from genotype. Progress addresses biology's central problem.

problems.title: Universal Biology
problems.tagline: Is Earth biology the only kind of life possible?
problems.summary: Characterize possible life forms beyond Earth biochemistry.
problems.description: Silicon-based life, non-water solvents, and alternative genetic polymers are speculated. With only Earth's n=1 example, universal biology remains speculative. Astrobiology missions may provide new data.
problems.abstract: This project pursues universal biology via (1) theoretical analysis of alternative biochemistries, (2) laboratory experiments with XNAs and non-water solvents, and (3) astrobiological implications for life detection. Progress frames biology's boundaries.

problems.title: Mechanism of Cambrian Explosion
problems.tagline: Why did complex life diversify rapidly ~541 million years ago?
problems.summary: Identify the drivers of the Cambrian explosion of animal diversity.
problems.description: Around 541 Mya, complex animal life diversified rapidly. Oxygen rise, predation emergence, Hox genes, and ecological triggers all have adherents. The specific drivers remain unresolved.
problems.abstract: We pursue Cambrian explosion drivers via (1) geochemical reconstruction of oxygen levels, (2) genomic comparison of basal metazoans, and (3) ecological models of trophic cascades. Progress addresses a fundamental evolutionary event.

problems.title: Is Aging Programmed or Accidental?
problems.tagline: Is aging the result of a program or accumulated damage?
problems.summary: Determine whether aging is evolutionarily programmed or an accidental consequence.
problems.description: Antagonistic pleiotropy, disposable soma, and mutation accumulation explain aging as accidental. Programmed death in some organisms suggests alternative. The answer shapes anti-aging strategy.
problems.abstract: This project examines aging causation via (1) comparative analysis across aging strategies, (2) experimental tests of programmed death mechanisms, and (3) mathematical models of evolutionary selection. Progress informs anti-aging approaches.

problems.title: Complete Brain Simulation
problems.tagline: Simulate a human brain in biological detail.
problems.summary: Achieve biophysically detailed simulation of a human brain.
problems.description: Blue Brain Project simulated a cortical column. Full human brain (86 billion neurons, 10¹⁵ synapses) requires extreme computational resources. Long-term feasibility is uncertain.
problems.abstract: We pursue brain simulation via (1) scaling algorithms for exascale computing, (2) biophysical model accuracy validation, and (3) integration of anatomical and physiological data. Progress tests computational limits.

problems.title: Protein Function from Sequence
problems.tagline: Predict protein function from sequence alone.
problems.summary: Achieve reliable protein function prediction from primary sequence.
problems.description: Structure is increasingly predictable but function does not follow directly. Allosteric regulation, context-dependence, and moonlighting proteins complicate function prediction.
problems.abstract: This project pursues function prediction via (1) deep learning models incorporating evolutionary and structural information, (2) functional genomics screens validating predictions, and (3) context-aware prediction frameworks. Progress transforms biology.

problems.title: Artificial Consciousness
problems.tagline: Can machines be conscious?
problems.summary: Determine whether artificial systems can be genuinely conscious.
problems.description: Artificial consciousness depends on solving the hard problem of consciousness. Chinese Room argument, substrate independence, and integrated information theory bear on the question. Progress may transcend current science.
problems.abstract: We examine artificial consciousness via (1) implementation of IIT measures in AI systems, (2) behavioral criteria for machine consciousness, and (3) philosophical analysis of substrate independence. Progress touches a deep question.

problems.title: Origin of Viruses
problems.tagline: Where did viruses come from?
problems.summary: Determine the evolutionary origins of viruses.
problems.description: Escaped genes, regressive cells, and pre-cellular origins are competing hypotheses. Polyphyletic origins are likely. Deep evolutionary history is largely lost. Progress informs the early biosphere.
problems.abstract: This project pursues virus origins via (1) phylogenomic analysis of viral lineages, (2) structural homology searches for ancestral motifs, and (3) theoretical models of early RNA world viruses. Progress clarifies viral biology.

problems.title: Ecosystem Prediction Under Climate Change
problems.tagline: Predict ecosystems under climate change.
problems.summary: Develop reliable ecosystem predictions under climate change scenarios.
problems.description: Nonlinear dynamics, tipping points, and novel ecosystems make ecosystem prediction difficult. Earth system models are incomplete. Progress is critical for climate adaptation planning.
problems.abstract: We pursue ecosystem prediction via (1) data-driven models trained on long-term ecosystem observations, (2) early warning signals for tipping points, and (3) integration with climate models. Progress supports adaptation.

problems.title: Biological Teleportation
problems.tagline: Copy molecular state to teleport biology.
problems.summary: Assess feasibility of complete molecular-level organism copying.
problems.description: Heisenberg uncertainty and the no-cloning theorem limit atomic-level copying. Classical state copying may be feasible but faces enormous practical and ethical challenges.
problems.abstract: This project examines biological teleportation via (1) theoretical analysis of copying requirements, (2) existing gene synthesis capabilities, and (3) ethical framework for conceivable implementations. Progress frames biology's computational limits.

problems.title: Intelligence in Other Forms
problems.tagline: What principles underlie convergent intelligence?
problems.summary: Characterize intelligence in octopus, corvids, and other non-mammals.
problems.description: Intelligence has evolved convergently in mammals, birds, and cephalopods. Octopus has a distributed nervous system. Universal principles of intelligence remain unknown.
problems.abstract: We pursue convergent intelligence via (1) comparative neuroanatomy across intelligent lineages, (2) behavioral studies of problem-solving, and (3) computational models of intelligence emergence. Progress clarifies cognition's foundations.

problems.title: Why Sexual Reproduction?
problems.tagline: Why does sexual reproduction exist given its twofold cost?
problems.summary: Explain the evolutionary maintenance of sexual reproduction.
problems.description: Red Queen, Muller's ratchet, and DNA repair hypotheses attempt to explain the maintenance of sexual reproduction despite its twofold cost. No single hypothesis fully accounts for the phenomenon.
problems.abstract: This project pursues sex evolution via (1) experimental tests of Red Queen dynamics, (2) Muller's ratchet analysis in asexual lineages, and (3) theoretical synthesis of competing hypotheses. Progress addresses a central evolutionary puzzle.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Ecological Resilience
problems.tagline: Predict regime shifts and recovery in ecosystems.
problems.summary: Develop a predictive framework for ecological resilience.
problems.description: Holling 1973 framed ecological resilience. Regime shifts and hysteresis characterize many ecosystems. Early warning signals are unreliable. A predictive framework is missing.
problems.abstract: We pursue ecological resilience theory via (1) analysis of long-term ecosystem time series, (2) early warning signal refinement, and (3) theoretical models of resilience. Progress informs conservation.

problems.title: Biological Basis of Altruism
problems.tagline: Explain extreme human cooperation evolutionarily.
problems.summary: Develop a unified theory of altruism from kin selection to human cooperation.
problems.description: Kin selection, reciprocal altruism, and group selection explain aspects of altruism. Human cooperation with strangers exceeds these frameworks' predictions. A unified theory is missing.
problems.abstract: This project pursues altruism theory via (1) experimental economics across cultures, (2) gene-culture coevolution models, and (3) comparative studies across cooperative species. Progress addresses human uniqueness.

problems.title: Mechanism of Mass Extinctions
problems.tagline: Explain and predict mass extinctions.
problems.summary: Characterize the mechanisms of Earth's five mass extinctions.
problems.description: K-Pg asteroid impact is clear. Permian, Devonian, and other extinctions involve volcanism, anoxia, and multiple causes. Prediction of future extinctions is limited.
problems.abstract: We pursue mass extinction mechanisms via (1) high-resolution geochemical reconstruction of extinction intervals, (2) comparative analysis of extinction drivers, and (3) modern biosphere vulnerability assessment. Progress informs conservation.

problems.title: Design Life from First Principles
problems.tagline: Design organisms bottom-up from first principles.
problems.summary: Achieve first-principles design of functional organisms.
problems.description: Bottom-up design rules are unknown. Emergent properties are unpredictable. Whole-cell models are primitive. First-principles organism design remains aspirational.
problems.abstract: This project pursues first-principles design via (1) whole-cell computational models, (2) synthetic circuit design principles, and (3) modular organism construction. Progress tests biology's predictability.

problems.title: Extraterrestrial Life Detection
problems.tagline: Detect life beyond Earth.
problems.summary: Develop reliable biosignature detection for extraterrestrial life.
problems.description: Mars, Europa, and Enceladus are targets for in-situ detection. Biosignatures are ambiguous. Remote detection via exoplanet atmospheres is limited. Discovery would transform humanity's perspective.
problems.abstract: We pursue extraterrestrial life detection via (1) refined biosignature criteria, (2) instrument development for in-situ missions, and (3) exoplanet atmosphere characterization. Progress addresses humanity's place in the cosmos.

problems.title: Biological Information Theory
problems.tagline: Unify semantic, functional, and regulatory biological information.
problems.summary: Develop a unified theory of biological information beyond Shannon.
problems.description: Shannon information is insufficient for biology. Semantic, functional, and regulatory information require new frameworks. A unified theory would clarify biology's informational nature.
problems.abstract: This project pursues biological information theory via (1) formal definitions of semantic and functional information, (2) algorithmic information applied to genomes, and (3) unified frameworks. Progress clarifies biology's foundations.

problems.title: Relationship Between Physics and Biology
problems.tagline: Is biology reducible to physics?
problems.summary: Address the relationship between physics and biology.
problems.description: Schrödinger's 1944 question remains unresolved. Emergence, downward causation, and the status of biological laws relative to physical ones are debated. Progress informs the architecture of science.
problems.abstract: We examine the physics-biology relationship via (1) case studies of emergent biological phenomena, (2) analysis of downward causation claims, and (3) theoretical frameworks for biological laws. Progress addresses science's foundations.
